seoTitle: How to Create an SMS Campaign With Brevo
metaDescription: Build and schedule an SMS marketing campaign in Brevo, from setting a sender ID to formatting character limits and running real device test sends.
intro: Broadcasting flash sales or event updates over text message means working within character formatting limits and carrier deliverability rules. Brevo's no-code SMS campaign builder handles the technical details, including compliance features and link shorteners, so you can put together a mobile campaign without manually managing phone records.

steps.heading: Launch a new SMS campaign
steps.body: Start a new SMS frame within the dashboard campaigns portal to begin building your message.

steps.heading: Set your sender ID
steps.body: Register a custom, alphanumeric Sender ID so recipients can clearly identify your brand in the text message.

steps.heading: Format your character string
steps.body: Write your message within the character allocation limits, keeping in mind that longer messages may split into multiple segments.

steps.heading: Embed dynamic attributes and opt-out text
steps.body: Insert dynamic contact attributes for personalization and include a localized opt-out mechanism to stay compliant.

steps.heading: Test and schedule the send
steps.body: Run a real-time smartphone test to confirm formatting, then time your final broadcast queue for delivery.

faq.question: What is a Sender ID and why does it matter?
faq.answer: A Sender ID is the custom alphanumeric name recipients see instead of a random phone number, helping with brand recognition and trust.

faq.question: Does a long SMS message cost more to send?
faq.answer: Messages exceeding the standard character limit typically split into multiple segments, which can affect cost depending on your plan.

faq.question: Is an opt-out option required in SMS campaigns?
faq.answer: Yes, including a localized opt-out mechanism is a standard compliance requirement for SMS marketing.

faq.question: Can I personalize SMS messages like emails?
faq.answer: Yes, Brevo supports embedding dynamic contact attributes into SMS messages for personalization.
